paper.title: Kinematics of High-Velocity Stars Utilizing LAMOST and Gaia DR3 Archives within 100 kpc

review.summary: Paper analyzes kinematics of three Programs of high-velocity stars (HiVels) using LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR3 within 100 kpc. Internal tables include fundamental parameters of 1197 HiVels (Programs I, II, III), velocity ellipsoid parameters and convergent points, velocity dispersion ratios, and Oort rotation constants. External resources point to LAMOST DR7, Gaia DR3 archive, and VizieR catalogs for the three HiVel programs. Tables t1, 007, and tspsig are LaTeX template artifacts without scientific content.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Fundamental parameters of 1197 HiVel stars from three Programs (I: 591 stars, II: 87 stars, III: 519 stars) compiled from literature catalogs.

source_refs.caption: The fundamental parameters of the three Programs I, II, and III of HiVels adopted by \cite{2021ApJS..252....3L,2023AJ....166...12L}, and \cite{2024AJ....167...76L}, respectively.
source_refs.label: tab1

internal_tables.evidence: The fundamental parameters of the three Programs I, II, and III of HiVels adopted by \cite{2021ApJS..252....3L,2023AJ....166...12L}, and \cite{2024AJ....167...76L}, respectively.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Velocity Ellipsoid Parameters (VEPs) and Convergent Points (CPs) computed for the three HiVel Programs.

source_refs.caption: VEPs and CPs of the three Program HiVels calculated by us.
source_refs.label: tab2

columns.name: Parameters
columns.meaning: Kinematic parameter name (spatial velocities, velocity dispersions, direction cosines, solar elements, convergent point)

columns.name: Program I
columns.meaning: Parameter value for Program I (591 HiVels, 0.10-15.40 kpc)

internal_tables.evidence: VEPs and CPs of the three Program HiVels calculated by us.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Ratios of velocity dispersions (sigma_2/sigma_1, sigma_3/sigma_1) and solar velocities for three HiVel Programs compared with disk and halo components from literature.

source_refs.caption: Ratios of velocity dispersions and the solar velocities for our three program HiVels and other components of the disks with different authors.
source_refs.label: tab3

columns.name: S_sun
columns.meaning: Solar velocity relative to the stellar group

columns.name: sigma_2/sigma_1
columns.meaning: Ratio of second to first principal velocity dispersion

columns.name: sigma_3/sigma_1
columns.meaning: Ratio of third to first principal velocity dispersion

internal_tables.evidence: Ratios of velocity dispersions and the solar velocities for our three program HiVels and other components of the disks with different authors.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Oort rotation constants (A, B), angular velocity, and rotational velocity computed from three HiVel Programs using radial velocity data.

source_refs.caption: Velocity dispersion and rotation constants for three program HiVels under study.
source_refs.label: tab4

columns.name: Program
columns.meaning: HiVel program identifier and distance range

columns.name: sigma_2/sigma_1
columns.meaning: Ratio of velocity dispersions used in Oort B calculation

columns.name: V_o
columns.meaning: Circular rotational velocity at solar radius

internal_tables.evidence: Velocity dispersion and rotation constants for three program HiVels under study.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Comparison of Oort constants and rotation parameters from this work with literature values using different methods and tracers.

source_refs.caption: Velocity dispersion and rotation constants for three program HiVels under study.
source_refs.label: tab5

columns.name: Methods
columns.meaning: Method used (radial velocity or proper motions)

internal_tables.evidence: Velocity dispersion and rotation constants for three program HiVels under study.

external_resources.description: Gaia DR3 data archive at ESAC.

source_refs.context:
\acknowledgements
% Do not leave a blank line here! <---------------------->
We sincerely thank the anonymous referee for their valuable suggestions, which have significantly improved the quality of this paper. This work presents results from the European Space Agency space mission Gaia. Gaia data are being processed by the Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC). Funding for the DPAC is provided by national institutions, in particular, the institutions participating in the Gaia MultiLateral Agreement (MLA). The Gaia mission website is \url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia}. The Gaia archive website is \url{https://archives.esac.esa.int/gaia}. The author extend their appreciation to the Deanship of Scientific Research at Northern Border University, Arar, KSA for funding this research work through the project number "NBU-FFR-2025-237-03”.

Guoshoujing Telescope (Large Sky Area Multi-Object Fiber Spectroscopic Telescope LAMOST) is a National Major Scientific Project built by the Chinese Academy of Sciences. Funding for the project has been provided by the National Development and Reform Commission. LAMOST is operated and managed by the National Astronomical Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences.\\
